rag_rules: Preserve existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place component type contracts in component type scopes
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections
Use valid component type scope for assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports
Use implementation scope for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain architectural integrity when inserting annex blocks
Preserve existing component hierarchy and connections
Use appropriate scope for each annex block type
Ensure inserted annex blocks are valid in their target scope
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless properly represented
Avoid placing component type contracts in implementation scopes
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax for assume, guarantee, assign, and validator diagnostics
